rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And For A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Her Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, January 28, 2025 Line 1. 1 - PLAINTIFF BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC.'s Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And For A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Her Counsel Of Record. Continued to March 3, 2025 on the Court's own motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS VS. LEICA MICROSYSTEMS INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Tuesday, January 28, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT LEICA MICROSYSTEMS INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. (CCP 437c(p)(2).) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). The evidence indicates that plaintiff is currently in good standing with the Franchise Tax Board and can therefore prosecute this action. (Paras Decl., pars. 11-13.) Defendant's reply fails to argue or show otherwise. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion calendar for Tuesday, January 28, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION's Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act. Plaintiff/petitioner American Accountability Foundation's petition for a writ of mandate is GRANTED. (The court's complete ruling is emailed to the parties.) On January 4, 2024, Plaintiff/petitioner American Accountability Foundation (Petitioner or AAF) asked Defendant/Respondent the Regents of the University of California (Respondent of Regents) to produce emails from October 7, 2023, forward involving UC San Francisco professor Dr. Rupa Marya that included the following keywords: "Israel, Palestine, Palestinian, Gaza, Hamas, Jew." Petitioner's request was made under the California Public Records Act (CPRA). The Regents responded explaining they had identified "19 pages as potentially responsive," but they were withholding 17 pages because "[they have] been determined to be exempt under Government Code section 7922.000 [in that] the public interest served by not disclosing clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure." "This includes," the Regents added, "records that are exempt due to the deliberative process privilege." The Regents provided redacted versions of the other two pages. Petitioner believes the withheld emails must be produced, hence this action under the CPRA. As relief, Petitioner requests: (1) a writ of mandate directing UCSF to comply with the (CPRA) and furnish all public documents meeting the description of the request, (2) declaratory relief that such records are subject to mandatory disclosure, (3) nominal damages, and (4) attorney's fees. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
